children.heading: Program
children.label: a Program
children.text: As part of the activities authorized under and , the Director shall carry out a fusion energy sciences research and enabling technology development program to effectively address the scientific and engineering challenges to building a cost competitive fusion power plant and to support the development of a competitive fusion power industry in the United States. As part of this program, the Director shall carry out research activities to expand the fundamental understandings of plasma and matter at very high temperatures and densities for fusion applications and for other engineering and plasma science applications.section 7139 of this titlesection 16312 of this title

children.heading: Fusion materials research and development
children.label: b Fusion materials research and development
A
children.text: the Director, in coordination with the Assistant Secretary for Nuclear Energy of the Department, shall carry out research and development activities to identify, characterize, and demonstrate materials that can endure the neutron, plasma, and heat fluxes expected in a fusion power system; and

children.label: i
children.text: the need for one or more facilities that can examine and test potential fusion and next generation fission materials and other enabling technologies relevant to the development of fusion power; and

children.label: ii
children.text: whether a single new facility that substantially addresses magnetic fusion and next generation fission materials research needs is feasible, in conjunction with the expected capabilities of facilities operational as of .September 28, 2018

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: Out of funds authorized to be appropriated under subsection (q), there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out activities described in paragraph (1) $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.1So in original. Probably should be “subsection (r),”. See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Tokamak research and development
children.label: c Tokamak research and development
children.text: The Director shall support research and development activities and facility operations to optimize the tokamak approach to fusion energy.

children.heading: Inertial fusion research and development
children.label: d Inertial fusion research and development

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall carry out a program of research and technology development in inertial fusion for energy applications, including ion beam, laser, and pulsed power fusion systems.

children.label: A
children.text: develop novel target designs;

children.label: B
children.text: support modeling of various inertial fusion energy concepts and systems;

children.label: C
children.text: develop diagnostic tools; and

children.label: D
children.text: improve inertial fusion energy driver technologies.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: Out of funds authorized to be appropriated under subsection (q), there are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the activities described in this subsection $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2027.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall support research and development activities and facility operations at institutions of higher education, National Laboratories, and private facilities in the United States for a portfolio of alternative and enabling fusion energy concepts that may provide solutions to significant challenges to the establishment of a commercial magnetic fusion power plant, prioritized based on the ability of the United States to play a leadership role in the international fusion research community.

children.label: i
children.text: advanced stellarator concepts;

children.label: ii
children.text: non-tokamak confinement configurations operating at low magnetic fields;

children.label: iii
children.text: magnetized target fusion energy concepts; or

children.label: iv
children.text: other promising fusion energy concepts identified by the Director;

children.label: i
children.text: high magnetic field approaches facilitated by high temperature superconductors;

children.label: ii
children.text: liquid metals to address issues associated with fusion plasma interactions with the inner wall of the encasing device; and

children.label: iii
children.text: advanced blankets for heat management and fuel breeding; and

children.label: C
children.text: advanced scientific computing activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Office of Science, shall support a program to provide fusion energy researchers with access to scientific and technical resources and expertise at facilities supported by the Department, including such facilities at National Laboratories and universities, to advance innovative fusion energy technologies toward commercial application.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be awarded on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may be in the form of grants, vouchers, equipment loans, or contracts to private entities.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: Out of funds authorized to be appropriated under subsection (q), there are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out the activities described in this subsection $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2021 through 2027.1
assess the potential for any fusion energy project supported by ARPA–E to represent a promising approach to a commercially viable fusion power plant;
determine whether the results of any fusion energy project supported by ARPA–E merit the support of follow-on research activities carried out by the Office of Science; and
avoid the unintentional duplication of activities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the fusion energy research and development activities that the Department proposes to carry out over the 10-year period following the date of the report under not fewer than 3 realistic budget scenarios, including a scenario based on 3-percent annual growth in the non-ITER portion of the budget for fusion energy research and development activities.September 28, 2018

children.label: i
children.text: identify specific areas of fusion energy research and enabling technology development in which the United States can and should establish or solidify a lead in the global fusion energy development effort;

children.label: ii
children.text: identify priorities for initiation of facility construction and facility decommissioning under each of the three budget scenarios described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: iii
children.text: assess the ability of the fusion workforce of the United States to carry out the activities identified under clauses (i) and (ii), including the adequacy of programs at institutions of higher education in the United States to train the leaders and workers of the next generation of fusion energy researchers.

children.heading: Process
children.label: 2 Process
children.text: In order to develop the report required under paragraph (1)(A), the Secretary shall leverage best practices and lessons learned from the process used to develop the most recent report of the Particle Physics Project Prioritization Panel of the High Energy Physics Advisory Panel.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 3 Requirement
children.text: No member of the Fusion Energy Sciences Advisory Committee shall be excluded from participating in developing or voting on final approval of the report required under paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Using the authority of the Secretary under , notwithstanding paragraph (10) of such section, the Secretary shall establish, not later than 6 months after the date of enactment of this section, a milestone-based fusion energy development program that requires projects to meet particular technical milestones before a participant is awarded funds by the Department.section 7256(g) of this title

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the program established by paragraph (1) shall be to support the development of a U.S.-based fusion power industry through the research and development of technologies that will enable the construction of new full-scale fusion systems capable of demonstrating significant improvements in the performance of such systems, as defined by the Secretary, within 10 years of the enactment of this section.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 3 Eligibility
children.text: Any entity is eligible to participate in the program provided that the Secretary has deemed it as having the necessary resources and expertise.

children.label: A
children.text: request proposals from eligible entities, as determined by the Secretary, that include proposed technical milestones, including estimated project timelines and total costs;

children.label: B
children.text: set milestones based on a rigorous technical review process;

children.label: C
children.text: award funding of a predetermined amount to projects that successfully meet proposed milestones under paragraph (1), or for expenses deemed reimbursable by the Secretary, in accordance with terms negotiated for an individual award; and

children.label: D
children.text: communicate regularly with selected eligible entities and, if the Secretary deems appropriate, exercise small amounts of flexibility for technical milestones as projects mature.

children.label: A
children.text: an award recipient shall be responsible for all costs until milestones are achieved, or reimbursable expenses are reviewed and verified by the Department;

children.label: B
children.text: should an awardee not meet the milestones described in paragraph (4), the Secretary may end the partnership with an award recipient and use the remaining funds in the ended agreement for new or existing projects carried out under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: consistent with the existing authorities of the Department, the Secretary may end the partnership with an award recipient for cause during the performance period.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 6 Applications
children.text: Any project proposal submitted to the program under paragraph (1) shall be evaluated based upon its scientific, technical, and business merits through a peer-review process, which shall include reviewers with appropriate expertise from the private sector, the investment community, and experts in the science and engineering of fusion and plasma physics.

children.heading: Project management
children.label: 7 Project management
children.text: In carrying out projects under this program and assessing the completion of their milestones in accordance with paragraph (4), the Secretary shall consult with experts that represent diverse perspectives and professional experiences, including those from the private sector, to ensure a complete and thorough review.

children.label: A
children.text: the benefits and drawbacks of a milestone-based fusion program as compared to traditional program structure funding models at the Department;

children.label: B
children.text: lessons-learned from program operations; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other matters the Secretary determines regarding the program.

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: 9 Annual report
children.text: As part of the annual budget request submitted for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall provide the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology and the Senate Committee on Energy and Natural Resources a report describing partnerships supported by the program established under paragraph (1) during the previous fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: $45,000,000 for fiscal year 2021;

children.label: B
children.text: $65,000,000 for fiscal year 2022;

children.label: C
children.text: $105,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: D
children.text: $65,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: E
children.text: $45,000,000 for fiscal year 2025;

children.label: F
children.text: $45,000,000 for fiscal year 2026; and

children.label: G
children.text: $45,000,000 for fiscal year 2027.

children.label: A
children.text: develop conceptual pilot plant designs and technology roadmaps; and

children.label: B
children.text: create an engineering design of a pilot plant that will bring fusion to commercial viability.

children.label: A
children.text: be composed of developers, manufacturers, universities, National Laboratories, and representatives of the engineering, procurement, and construction industries; and

children.label: B
children.text: include public-private partnerships.

children.text: $35,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: B

children.label: E

children.heading: General plasma science and applications
children.label: k General plasma science and applications
children.text: The Director shall support research in general plasma science and high energy density physics that advance the understanding of the scientific community of fundamental properties and complex behavior of matter to control and manipulate plasmas for a broad range of applications, including support for research relevant to advancements in chip manufacturing and microelectronics.

children.heading: Sense of Congress
children.label: l Sense of Congress
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the United States should support a robust, diverse program in addition to providing sufficient support to, at a minimum, meet its commitments to ITER and maintain the schedule of the project as determined by the Secretary in coordination with the ITER Organization at the time of the enactment of this section. It is further the sense of Congress that developing the scientific basis for fusion, providing research results key to the success of ITER, and training the next generation of fusion scientists are of critical importance to the United States and should in no way be diminished by participation of the United States in the ITER project.

as practicable and in coordination with other appropriate Federal agencies as necessary, ensure the access of United States researchers to the most advanced fusion research facilities and research capabilities in the world, including ITER;

to the maximum extent practicable, continue to leverage United States participation ITER, and prioritize expanding international partnerships and investments in current and future fusion research facilities within the United States; and2So in original.2

to the maximum extent practicable, prioritize engagement in collaborative efforts in support of future international facilities that would provide access to the most advanced fusion research facilities in the world to United States researchers.

children.heading: Fission and fusion research coordination report
children.label: n Fission and fusion research coordination report

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall transmit to Congress a report addressing opportunities for coordinating fusion energy research and development activities between the Office of Nuclear Energy, the Office of Science, and the Advanced Research Projects Agency—Energy.

children.label: A
children.text: liquid metals to address issues associated with fusion plasma interactions with the inner wall of the encasing device and other components within the reactor;

children.label: B
children.text: immersion blankets for heat management and fuel breeding;

children.label: C
children.text: technologies and methods for instrumentation and control;

children.label: D
children.text: computational methods and codes for system operation and maintenance;

children.label: E
children.text: codes and standard development;

children.label: F
children.text: radioactive waste handling;

children.label: G
children.text: radiological safety;

children.label: H
children.text: potential for non-electricity generation applications; and

children.label: I
children.text: any other overlapping priority as identified by the Director of the Office of Science or the Assistant Secretary of Energy for Nuclear Energy.

children.heading: High-performance computation collaborative research program
children.label: o High-performance computation collaborative research program
A
children.text: to support fundamental research in plasmas and matter at very high temperatures and densities;

children.label: B
children.text: to inform the development of a broad range of fusion energy systems; and

children.label: C
children.text: to facilitate the translation of research results in fusion energy science to industry.

children.label: A
children.text: To use expertise from the private sector, institutions of higher education, and the National Laboratories to leverage existing, and develop new, computational software and capabilities that prospective users may use to accelerate research and development of fusion energy systems.

children.label: B
children.text: To develop computational tools to simulate and predict fusion energy science phenomena that may be validated through physical experimentation.

children.label: C
children.text: To increase the utility of the research infrastructure of the Department by coordinating with the Advanced Scientific Computing Research program within the Office of Science.

children.label: D
children.text: To leverage experience from existing modeling and simulation entities sponsored by the Department.

children.label: E
children.text: To ensure that new experimental and computational tools are accessible to relevant research communities, including private sector entities engaged in fusion energy technology development.

children.label: F
children.text: To ensure that newly developed computational tools are compatible with modern virtual engineering and visualization capabilities to accelerate the realization of fusion energy technologies and systems.

children.label: A
children.text: other research entities of the Department, including the National Laboratories, the Advanced Research Projects Agency—Energy, and the Advanced Scientific Computing Research program within the Office of Science; and

children.label: B
children.text: industry.

children.heading: High-Performance Computing for Fusion Innovation Center
children.label: 4 High-Performance Computing for Fusion Innovation Center

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the program under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall, in coordination with the Innovation Network for Fusion Energy, establish and operate a national High-Performance Computing for Fusion Innovation Center (referred to in this paragraph as the “Center”), to support the program under paragraph (1) by providing, to the extent practicable, a centralized entity for multidisciplinary, collaborative, fusion energy research and development through high-performance computing and advanced data analytics technologies and processes.

children.label: i
children.text: a National Laboratory;

children.label: ii
children.text: an institution of higher education;

children.label: iii
children.text: a multi-institutional collaboration; or

children.label: iv
children.text: any other entity that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Application
children.label: i Application
children.text: To be eligible to serve as the Center, an eligible entity shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Selection
children.label: ii Selection
children.text: The Secretary shall select the Center on a competitive, merit-reviewed basis.

children.heading: Existing activities
children.label: D Existing activities
children.text: The Center may incorporate existing research activities that are consistent with the program under paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Center shall prioritize activities that utilize expertise and infrastructure from a balance among the private sector, institutions of higher education, and the National Laboratories to enhance existing computation tools and develop new computational software and capabilities to accelerate the commercial application of fusion energy systems.

children.heading: Maintenance of resource availability
children.label: ii Maintenance of resource availability
children.text: The Secretary may enter into contracts with commercial cloud computing providers to ensure that resource availability within the Department is not reduced or disproportionately distributed as a result of Center activities.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: F Duration
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (G), the Center shall receive support for a period of not more than 5 years, subject to the availability of appropriations.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: G Renewal
children.text: On the expiration of the period of support of the Center under subparagraph (F), the Secretary may renew support for the Center, on a merit-reviewed basis, for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall construct a Material Plasma Exposure Experiment facility as described in the 2020 publication approved by the Fusion Energy Sciences Advisory Committee entitled “Powering the Future: Fusion and Plasmas”. The Secretary shall consult with the private sector, institutions of higher education, National Laboratories, and relevant Federal agencies to ensure that the facility is capable of meeting Federal research needs for steady state, high-heat-flux, and plasma-material interaction testing of fusion materials over a range of fusion energy relevant parameters.

children.label: A
children.text: A magnetic field at the target of 1 Tesla.

children.label: B
children.text: An energy flux at the target of 10 MW/m.2

children.label: C
children.text: The ability to expose previously irradiated plasma facing material samples to plasma.

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: 3 Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the facility described in paragraph (1) occurs before .December 31, 2027

children.label: A
children.text: $21,895,000 for fiscal year 2023; and

children.label: B
children.text: $3,800,000 for fiscal year 2024.

children.heading: Matter in Extreme Conditions instrument upgrade
children.label: q Matter in Extreme Conditions instrument upgrade

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the upgrade to the Matter in Extreme Conditions endstation at the Linac Coherent Light Source as described in the 2020 publication approved by the Fusion Energy Sciences Advisory Committee entitled “Powering the Future: Fusion and Plasmas”. The Secretary shall consult with the private sector, institutions of higher education, National Laboratories, and relevant Federal agencies to ensure that this facility is capable of meeting Federal research needs for understanding physical and chemical changes to plasmas at fundamental timescales, and explore new regimes of dense material physics, astrophysics, planetary physics, and short-pulse laser-plasma interactions.

children.heading: Start of operations
children.label: 2 Start of operations
children.text: The Secretary shall, subject to the availability of appropriations, ensure that the start of full operations of the facility described in paragraph (1) occurs before .December 31, 2028
